topic: Preet's first school play — she was a tree

turns.content: mate you will not believe what happened today. Preet was in her first school play and she was a TREE. an actual tree. and she absolutely smashed it
Ha, that's amazing! Being a tree in a school play is a genuine rite of passage. I love that she smashed it — how exactly does one smash it as a tree? Was there some serious dramatic swaying involved?

mate she took it SO seriously. she had branches made out of cardboard on her arms and she was waving them about like she was in a storm. the teacher said she was supposed to just stand there but Preet went full method actor. the other parents were cracking up

Full method acting as a tree — that's genuinely brilliant. She saw a static background role and decided it needed more drama. Honestly, that kind of commitment at her age is really entertaining. Did she have any lines or was it purely a physical performance?

no lines at all that was the best part. she was literally just supposed to stand in the background as part of the forest scene. but she was doing sound effects and everything. making whooshing noises with her mouth. Simran was trying not to laugh and I was filming the whole thing on my phone

The unauthorized sound effects are the best part. She created her own foley department at age five. Please tell me you got the whooshing on camera — that's the kind of footage you bring out at her 18th birthday party.

What was the play actually about? Was she upstaging the main characters with her tree performance?

it was like a nature story thing, something about animals in a forest and looking after the environment. the main characters were a fox and a badger I think. but honestly no one was watching the fox and badger cos Preet's tree was stealing the show. Arjun was sat next to me going 'why is she doing that' haha

An environmental play where the tree outperforms the fox and the badger — there's a beautiful irony there. The trees ARE the environment, so really Preet was the most important character and she knew it.

Arjun's reaction is perfect too. Older sibling embarrassment kicking in already. Did the teacher say anything afterwards about Preet's... creative interpretation?

the teacher was proper nice about it actually. she came over after and said Preet has great enthusiasm and a big personality. which is teacher code for 'your daughter went completely off script' innit. but she was smiling so I think she found it funny. Simran asked if they do drama club and the teacher said not till year 1 but Preet would be perfect for it

"Great enthusiasm and a big personality" is absolutely teacher diplomacy for "she did her own thing entirely" — but the fact that the teacher recommended drama club means Preet genuinely made an impression. That's a good sign. Some kids freeze up on stage, and yours was adding her own sound design.

Drama club in year 1 could be brilliant for her. Give her an actual speaking role and she'll probably direct the whole production by the end of it.
honestly I wouldn't put it past her. she's got that energy innit. we went to McDonald's after to celebrate and she kept telling the person at the counter she was a tree today. the guy was so confused

Walking into McDonald's and announcing to the staff that she was a tree today — that's the kind of confidence most adults wish they had. Did Arjun get into it at all or was he doing the embarrassed older brother thing the whole time?

he was embarrassed at the play but by McDonalds he was laughing about it too. he kept doing impressions of her whooshing noises on the way home and she was getting proper wound up going 'thats not what I sounded like!!' typical siblings innit

Classic sibling dynamic — he waits until the spotlight is off and then roasts her for it in the car. The fact that she was offended about the accuracy of his impression rather than the teasing itself says everything about Preet. She's committed to the craft.

Did Simran manage to keep it together through the whole performance or did she crack?

Simran was biting her lip the whole time trying to be a proper supportive mum and not laugh. she held it together until the whooshing noises started and then she had to look at the floor. brilliant evening though mate. proper proud dad moment

Simran deserves credit for lasting that long — the whooshing noises would break anyone. Sounds like a really lovely evening, the whole family cracking up at Preet's theatrical debut as an overachieving tree. Those are the memories that stick.

And she'll only get more confident from here, so brace yourself for the year 1 drama club audition monologue. Enjoy the video — that one's a keeper.
